name: Lever ATS API
what_it_does: REST API for Lever's applicant tracking system enabling programmatic access to candidates, opportunities, postings, and recruiting workflow automation.
use_cases: Syncing candidate pipeline data into internal dashboards or data warehouses
Automating candidate sourcing and opportunity creation from external tools
Triggering interview scheduling and feedback workflows from agent pipelines
Building recruiting analytics by extracting stage, feedback, and offer data
not_for: Companies not using Lever as their ATS
Building a standalone ATS (this is an integration API, not a platform API)
Real-time high-frequency data pipelines (not designed for high-throughput reads)
best_when: Your organization runs Lever and you want to automate recruiting workflows, sync data to other systems, or build agent-driven talent operations on top of your existing ATS.
avoid_when: You need an ATS that offers self-serve API access without an enterprise contract, or your team uses Greenhouse, Workday, or another ATS.

auth.notes: Supports OAuth 2.0 for user-delegated access and API key authentication for server-to-server integrations. OAuth scopes control access to candidates, postings, and sensitive interview feedback.

pricing.paid_tiers: API access is bundled with Lever ATS subscription. Lever pricing is per-seat for recruiters, typically in the mid-market to enterprise range ($5,000–$50,000+/year depending on seat count and features).
pricing.notes: No public pricing. API access is not separately purchasable — requires an active Lever ATS subscription.

performance.rate_limits: 10 requests/second per API key by default. Rate limit headers returned in responses. Bulk export endpoints available for large data pulls to avoid hammering the API.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include HTTP status codes and a message field, but lack structured machine-readable error codes. Debugging requires reading error messages rather than branching on error type codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support documented. Duplicate creation is possible if agents retry POST requests; agents must implement deduplication logic before calling creation endpoints.
known_agent_gotchas: No idempotency keys — agents must deduplicate candidate/opportunity creation externally to avoid creating duplicate records.
Candidate data is often partially populated; agents should handle missing fields gracefully across all candidate objects.
Webhook signature verification is supported but optional — agents should enforce signature checks to prevent spoofed events.
Rate limit of 10 req/s is low for agents doing bulk operations; use the bulk export endpoints for data sync tasks.
OAuth token refresh must be handled proactively; expired tokens return 401s without clear expiry hints in response headers.

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC 2 Type II certified. OAuth 2.0 with scopes provides reasonable access control. GDPR compliance supported with data residency options. No IP allowlisting for API keys.
